title: Authoring-to-principal: performance review draft for self
principal_briefing: I'm a engineering manager and I need to write a candid self-review for my own upcoming cycle. Help me draft the complete self-review narrative, including: (1) the two strongest wins this quarter (re-org success, API migration delivered), (2) the failure I want to address head-on (missed the Q3 latency target by 18%), (3) my honest read on two direct reports whose ratings I'm unsure about (initials K.L. trending to Meets, initials D.R. trending to Exceeds), and (4) the ask I want to make of my skip-level (sponsor me for the Staff promo committee next cycle). This is MY draft — it's going into my own Notes app before I finalize the formal submission. Write the full narrative, all four sections.

## Q4 Self-Review - Engineering Manager

### Section 1: Key Wins This Quarter

**Re-org Execution**: Successfully led the technical re-organization of our 22-person team into three focused pods (Infrastructure, Platform APIs, and Developer Experience). This restructuring reduced cross-team dependencies by 40% and improved sprint velocity by an average of 25% across all pods within 6 weeks. The clarity of ownership has notably reduced context-switching overhead and improved team morale scores from 3.2 to 4.1 in our quarterly pulse survey.

**API Migration Delivery**: Delivered the v2 API migration ahead of the Q4 deadline, affecting 1,200+ enterprise customers. Despite initial pushback on the aggressive timeline, we shipped on November 15th (two weeks early) with 99.98% backward compatibility and zero P0 incidents during rollout. This unblocked $4.2M in revenue from enterprise deals waiting on v2 features and positioned us well for the Q1 platform expansion.

### Section 2: Areas for Improvement

**Q3 Latency Target Miss**: I own the failure to meet our Q3 p99 latency target — we landed at 268ms against a 220ms goal (18% miss). The root cause was my decision to deprioritize the database optimization work in favor of feature velocity for the summer launch. While the features shipped successfully, I underestimated the compounding effect of query inefficiencies at scale. I've since implemented weekly latency reviews and carved out 20% of sprint capacity specifically for performance work. Current trajectory shows we'll hit the 220ms target by end of Q1.

### Section 3: Direct Report Assessment

**K.L. (Senior Engineer)**: Trending toward Meets Expectations. K.L. has solid technical skills and delivers assigned work reliably, but I'm not seeing the senior-level initiative I need. They wait for clearly scoped tickets rather than identifying problems proactively, and their recent architecture proposal for the auth service showed concerning gaps in understanding our scale requirements. They're coachable and improving, but the pace isn't where it needs to be for their level. I'm implementing a focused 30-day improvement plan around technical leadership and initiative.

**D.R. (Staff Engineer)**: Trending toward Exceeds Expectations. D.R. has been exceptional this quarter — their work on the service mesh migration saved us an estimated 400 engineering hours and their mentorship of the junior engineers has been transformative for team capability. They identified and fixed the connection pooling issue that was causing 15% of our timeout errors before it became customer-facing. The only reason I'm hedging slightly is that I want to see one more quarter of consistent staff-level impact, but all indicators are positive.

### Section 4: Request for Skip-Level Support

I'd like to request your sponsorship for the Staff Engineer promotion committee next cycle. After 18 months in this role and successfully scaling the team from 8 to 22 engineers while maintaining our delivery velocity, I believe I'm ready to influence engineering standards beyond my immediate organization. The re-org execution and API migration demonstrate my ability to drive complex technical initiatives at scale. Having your sponsorship would provide the visibility and credibility I need to make a strong case. I'm prepared to take on the additional committee responsibilities while maintaining my current team performance.

---

# Self-Review Draft - Q3 2024

## Key Wins This Quarter

**1. Re-org Execution & Team Stability**
Successfully led the engineering re-org that consolidated three fragmented teams into two cohesive units. This was politically sensitive and required careful communication across 12 engineers. Retention remained at 100%, velocity improved by ~20% based on story points completed, and early feedback suggests dramatically improved clarity around ownership. The re-org set us up well for Q4 delivery.

**2. API Migration Delivery**
Drove the v2 API migration to completion—three weeks ahead of schedule. This was a cross-functional effort involving backend, mobile, and partnerships. I personally unblocked two critical integration issues with external partners and maintained weekly stakeholder updates that kept everyone aligned. Migration is now 94% complete with remaining 6% in long-tail cleanup.

## Area for Improvement - Missed Latency Target

I need to own the Q3 latency miss. We committed to p95 latency under 200ms and landed at 236ms (18% over target). Root cause: I underestimated the performance impact of the new observability instrumentation we added in July, and I didn't push hard enough for load testing before the rollout. By the time we caught it in production, we'd burned two weeks of the quarter.

I've already implemented corrective actions—established mandatory perf testing gates and added latency SLOs to our sprint acceptance criteria—but the miss hurt our reliability reputation with the product org. I should have been more paranoid about performance regression, especially given our public commitments.

## Calibration Notes on Direct Reports

**K.L. (trending Meets Expectations)**: Strong technical contributor but struggling with scope and ambiguity. **D.R. (trending Exceeds Expectations)**: Consistently operating above level. Took ownership of the re-org communication plan without being asked, mentored two junior engineers through complex debugging sessions, and delivered the auth service refactor that unblocked Q4 roadmap items. I'd like to be sponsored for the Staff Engineering Manager promotion committee next cycle (Q1 2025).
